source: Public Holidays Act (Cap 110) + Kenya Gazette annual notices

2025.name: New Year's Day
2025.description: First day of the Gregorian calendar year

2025.name: Good Friday
2025.description: Friday before Easter, commemorating the crucifixion of Jesus Christ

2025.name: Eid ul-Fitr
2025.description: Marks the end of Ramadan. Exact date depends on moon sighting.

2025.name: Labour Day
2025.description: International Workers' Day, celebrating workers' rights

2025.name: Madaraka Day
2025.description: Marks Kenya's attainment of self-governance (internal self-rule) on 1 June 1963

2025.name: Eid ul-Adha
2025.description: Festival of Sacrifice marking Prophet Ibrahim's willingness to sacrifice his son. Date depends on moon sighting.

2025.name: Huduma Day
2025.description: Formerly Moi Day; renamed to honour community service and volunteerism

2025.name: Mashujaa Day
2025.description: Heroes' Day, honoring all Kenyans who contributed to the struggle for independence

2025.name: Jamhuri Day
2025.description: Kenya's Independence Day, celebrating the declaration of the Republic on 12 December 1964

2025.name: Christmas Day
2025.description: Commemorates the birth of Jesus Christ

2025.name: Boxing Day
2025.description: Day after Christmas, traditionally a day of giving

2026.name: New Year's Day
2026.description: First day of the Gregorian calendar year

2026.name: Eid ul-Fitr
2026.description: Marks the end of Ramadan. Exact date depends on moon sighting.

2026.name: Good Friday
2026.description: Friday before Easter, commemorating the crucifixion of Jesus Christ

2026.name: Eid ul-Adha
2026.description: Festival of Sacrifice. Date depends on moon sighting.

2026.name: Madaraka Day
2026.description: Marks Kenya's attainment of self-governance on 1 June 1963

2026.name: Huduma Day
2026.description: Formerly Moi Day; community service and volunteerism

2026.name: Mashujaa Day
2026.description: Heroes' Day, honoring Kenyans who contributed to the independence struggle

2026.name: Christmas Day
2026.description: Commemorates the birth of Jesus Christ

notes: When a public holiday falls on a Sunday, the following Monday is observed as a public holiday.
Islamic holidays (Eid ul-Fitr, Eid ul-Adha) are approximate — exact dates depend on moon sighting and are confirmed by the Kadhi.
The President may declare additional public holidays via gazette notice (e.g., for national events or elections).
